name: Cassata Cake
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. In a large bowl, beat together 1 cup sugar and the butter for about 7 minutes, or until very light and fluffy. Add egg yolks one at a time, beating well after each addition. Continue beating for another 5 minutes, or until very fluffy.
In a separate bowl, sift together cake flour, baking powder, and salt. Alternately add dry ingredients and milk to the creamed mixture, beating well after each addition. Wash beaters thoroughly.
Beat egg whites with a mixer until soft peaks form. Gradually add the remaining 3/4 cup sugar, continuing to beat until stiff peaks form. Gently fold the whipped egg whites into the creamed mixture. Pour batter into an ungreased 10-inch tube pan and bake for 50 to 55 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Invert the cake onto a wire rack and let cool completely in the pan. Remove from the pan once cooled. Cut horizontally into three layers. Spread Orange-Ricotta Filling between layers and frost the top and sides of the cake with Vanilla Frosting, sprinkling chopped nuts around the edge of the top. Chill before serving.
Orange-Ricotta Filling: In a saucepan, combine sugar and cornstarch. Stir in milk all at once. Cook over medium heat until the mixture thickens and bubbles, then cook for an additional 2 minutes while stirring constantly. Remove from heat and cover with plastic wrap to prevent skin from forming. Cool completely without stirring. In a mixing bowl, beat ricotta cheese until creamy. Fold in the cooled sugar mixture, orange liqueur, and vanilla extract. Stir in chocolate and candied citron. Makes about 3 cups.
Vanilla Frosting: Cream together shortening and vanilla in a small bowl. Gradually add powdered sugar, beating until smooth. Beat in milk as needed to achieve a spreading consistency. Use immediately to frost the cake.
